name: Geshtinanna
description: Geshtinanna, also known as Gestinanna or Ngeshtinanna, is a prominent Sumerian goddess associated with agriculture, specifically viticulture (winemaking), dream interpretation, and scribal arts. She is the sister of Dumuzi (Tammuz) and is often depicted as a kind and compassionate figure.  Her origins are rooted in the agricultural practices of ancient Mesopotamia, where the cultivation of grapes and the production of wine were vital aspects of their economy and culture. Geshtinanna's role is crucial in the myth of Dumuzi's death and resurrection.  After Dumuzi's death, Geshtinanna volunteered to spend half of each year in the underworld in his place, allowing him to return to the world of the living for the other half, thus representing the cycle of the seasons. This act of self-sacrifice highlights her loyalty and love for her brother. Her association with dream interpretation stems from her ability to access the underworld and gain knowledge of the future. As a patron of scribes, she is linked to wisdom, literacy, and the preservation of knowledge, reflecting the importance of written records in Sumerian society. She is a symbol of fertility, sacrifice, and the enduring power of familial love.
subtitle: Goddess of Vines, Dreams, and Scribes

keyMyths.title: Dumuzi's Descent
keyMyths.description: After Dumuzi's death, Geshtinanna offers to take his place in the underworld for half the year. This act allows Dumuzi to return to the world of the living, symbolizing the cycle of seasons.

keyMyths.title: Geshtinanna as Scribe
keyMyths.description: Geshtinanna is revered as a patron goddess of scribes and writing, representing wisdom and literacy. She is associated with the preservation of knowledge and the importance of written records.

keyMyths.title: Dream Interpretation
keyMyths.description: Geshtinanna's connection to the underworld gives her the ability to interpret dreams and offer insight into the future. People sought her guidance for understanding their dreams.

sources.author: Samuel Noah Kramer
sources.work: The Sumerians: Their History, Culture, and Character
sources.description: Provides extensive information on Sumerian mythology and culture.
sources.text: Provides extensive information on Sumerian mythology and culture.

sources.author: Diane Wolkstein and Samuel Noah Kramer
sources.work: Inanna: Queen of Heaven and Earth
sources.description: Offers translations and interpretations of Sumerian myths, including those involving Geshtinanna.
sources.text: Offers translations and interpretations of Sumerian myths, including those involving Geshtinanna.
